commit.message:
Add bridge server, mobile UI, and CLI for monitoring Claude Code from phone

Hook-based architecture using Claude Code's HTTP hooks to stream tool calls,
errors, and notifications to a phone via WebSocket. Includes chat-back feature
that injects messages through the Stop hook.

Bundle contextual photos per step: bikes for The Beginning, runs for
Crossfit to Running, mountains for Discovering Hiking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: fix journey gallery layout collapsing on mobile

Move aspect ratio to wrapper div with absolute-positioned scroll
container inside, and use flex-none + explicit h-full/w-full on
slides so images size correctly on all screen widths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add w-full to timeline columns for mobile layout

The md:w-1/2 divs had no width on mobile, causing the gallery
(which uses w-full + aspect ratio) to collapse to zero width
under items-start flex alignment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add peek-scroll gallery and all hiking photos

Rework JourneyStepGallery to show adjacent photos peeking from the
sides (24px peek + 8px gap) so users know they can scroll. Move dot
indicators below the image. Add all 7 mountain photos to the
Discovering Hiking timeline step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: rework gallery to card stack with slanted side cards

Center image sits on top (z-10), prev/next cards peek from behind
with 4deg rotation, 92% scale, and a dark overlay. Clicking a side
card or using arrows/dots animates the transition (500ms ease-out).
No scroll-based logic — pure state-driven with CSS transitions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add touch swipe and fix card stack overflow

Add touch swipe support (50px threshold) on the center card for
mobile navigation. Reduce side card size (80% width, 3deg rotation,
0.9 scale) and add px-4 padding to prevent cards from protruding
outside the container.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: fluid drag-to-swipe for card stack gallery

Center card follows finger in real-time during drag. Side cards
interpolate rotation/scale/overlay toward center as you drag toward
them. Releases use velocity + distance threshold to decide snap vs
bounce-back. Easing uses cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for a
natural spring-like settle. Rubber-band effect at edges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: feat: LUT filters on video, grid display, baked export, new presets - Extend LutFilterCanvas with srcType="video" prop: uses an internal HTMLVideoElement + requestAnimationFrame loop to upload each decoded frame to the WebGL TEXTURE_2D and redraw through the 3D LUT every tick. A playing prop (default true) lets callers pause/resume the video. - Add src/lib/lutExport.ts (LutExporter class): off-screen WebGL2 canvas with preserveDrawingBuffer=true that bakes the LUT into exported media. Image export renders the source through WebGL then blits to a 2D canvas with manual-adjustment CSS filters on top. Video export creates the exporter once before the frame loop and calls render() per seek frame, covering both the WebCodecs and MediaRecorder paths. - Fix SortableMediaItem grid thumbnails: detect active LUT preset and render LutFilterCanvas (image or video) instead of img/video so the colour grade is visible in the grid after closing Studio. - Fix canvas cropping in Studio preview: pass style={{ height: 'auto' }} to the main-preview LutFilterCanvas so its CSS height scales proportionally with max-width (matching behaviour) instead of defaulting to the large canvas.height attribute value. - Add Portra 160, Fuji Film, and Moody Blue LUT presets to data.ts and include the corresponding .cube files in public/. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

commit.message:
Fix 3 conversion bugs and add batch report

- Section splitting: when TOC anchor IDs don't exist in the HTML (a
  common EPUB defect), fall back to matching section boundaries by
  title text instead of silently returning the full file for every
  sub-section entry.

- CSS heading detection: ignore compound/descendant CSS selectors
  (e.g. "#id .classname") when detecting heading classes, preventing
  normal body paragraph styles from being promoted to headings.

- Batch mode: add a summary report at the end showing converted /
  skipped / warned / errored counts, with per-file warning counts and
  error messages. Errors are highlighted in red. UTF-8 is forced on
  Windows console streams so accented titles display correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Sett NumberOfChannels direkte, unngå _safe_set klamping

_safe_set() klampa NumberOfChannels til prop.max_value (C++ RefDevice
standard), so berre N-1 kanalar vart oppretta sjølv om N var konfigurert.
OPC-UA-noden vart fiksa til N, men C++ OPC-UA-treet hadde berre N-1 nodar.
No brukar vi set_property_value() direkte utan range-klamping.

Convert EduRate to single-org UWC version

- Add DB migration to remap existing old role values
- Remove email verification from student/teacher registration (direct signup)
- Rewrite join.html (teacher) and register.html (student) to single-step forms
- Remove routes/apply.js and routes/organizations.js (multi-org endpoints)
- Add clickable org name badge in topbar (admin can rename org inline)
- Add PUT /api/admin/org endpoint for org rename
- Fix editUser role dropdown to use correct new role values (head, not school_head)
- Seed default accounts: admin/head/teacher/student @uwc.edu
- Remove multi-org features from landing.html (apply form, multi-org stat/feature)
- Update landing page copy for UWC

fix: ThreadPoolExecutor 제거로 무한 스피너 데드락 해결

백그라운드 스레드에서 @st.cache_data + st.error() 호출 시
Streamlit 컨텍스트 접근 대기 → 메인 스레드 deadlock 발생.
외부 ThreadPoolExecutor를 제거하고 순차 로드로 변경.

commit.message:
Add interactive proof graph visualization to explorer

- DAG layout: premises on left, derivations flow right to conclusion
- Color-coded by inference rule type (MP=green, MT=red, DS=orange, etc.)

commit.message:
Production-hardening: packaging, type hints, error handling, tests, CI, Thessaloniki network

Packaging
- Replace requirements.txt with PEP 621 pyproject.toml (pinned deps with
  upper bounds, [dev] extras for pytest/mypy/ruff, py-modules for flat layout)
- Add py.typed marker (PEP 561 compliance)

Type hints & error handling
- Add from __future__ import annotations and module-level loggers to all four
  core modules (runner, risk_model, accident_manager, metrics)
- Replace every bare except: pass with logger.warning/debug calls that include
  vehicle ID / accident ID / step context
- Fix datetime.utcnow() deprecation → datetime.now(timezone.utc)

Tests (55 unit tests, no SUMO required)
- tests/conftest.py: session-scoped TraCI mock injected into sys.modules
- tests/test_risk_model.py: 17 tests (Nilsson model, variance, density, trigger)
- tests/test_accident_manager.py: 14 tests (lifecycle, severity distribution)
- tests/test_metrics.py: 15 tests (AI computation, export, snapshots)
- tests/test_config_validation.py: 9 tests (all validation failure paths)

CI/CD
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: lint (ruff + mypy) + pytest matrix on
  Python 3.10 / 3.11 / 3.12; unit tests run without SUMO in CI

Network generation
- generate_thessaloniki.py: downloads Thessaloniki OSM via Overpass API,
  converts with netconvert, generates 7 168 routes, writes .sumocfg, and
  optionally patches config.yaml (--update-config / --gui flags)
- config.yaml updated to point at Thessaloniki network (smoke-tested: 600
  steps, 3 accidents, 520 vehicles — confirmed working)
- base_probability calibrated for Thessaloniki (~500 vehicles): 5.0e-05

README
- Add CI / Python 3.10+ / MIT badges
- New "Bundled Networks" section documenting all three generators with
  quick-start commands for Thessaloniki, Sioux Falls, and Riverside District
- New calibration table: base_probability vs simultaneous vehicle count
- Development section: note 55-test suite and TraCI mock strategy
- Installation: pip install . / pip install -e ".[dev]" workflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

audit kmp roadmap AjuNk (#115)

* fix(backend): add missing install import to all 3 Application.kt files

All three backend services (api, license, sync) failed Docker image
builds with 'Unresolved reference: install' because
io.ktor.server.application.install was not explicitly imported.
In Ktor 3.x the install extension function must be imported separately
from the Application class itself.

Fixes build-backend-images CI failure (SHA 0c29487 / run 22708434211).

https://claude.ai/code/session_0195xibztDGTtaHvqNKVstKz

* fix(sync): use pingPeriodMillis/timeoutMillis in WebSockets config (Ktor 3.x)

Ktor 3.x renamed the WebSocket plugin configuration properties:
- pingPeriod  → pingPeriodMillis (Long, milliseconds)
- timeout     → timeoutMillis    (Long, milliseconds)

The old Duration-based names don't exist in WebSocketOptions for
Ktor 3.4.1, causing 'Unresolved reference' compile errors in the
build-backend-images (sync) CI job.

Removes the unused kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.seconds import.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0195xibztDGTtaHvqNKVstKz

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove non-functional vSyncCount interceptor. Add preferredRefreshRate to resolution
patches for proper high-refresh-rate support. Add solution file and README.

PeerTube, and Elk. Supports both fresh installation via debconf preseeding
and targeted apt upgrades of existing deployments.

  install, upgrade + status endpoints)
- templates/jitsi.html: two-column Bootstrap 5 dark theme config/status UI

  notifier)

Modified files:
- app.py: register jitsi blueprint
- routes/applications.py + templates/applications.html: add Jitsi card
- notifier.py: add Jitsi Discord notifications
- scheduler.py: add periodic jitsi_check job
